DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT GEORGE PFLEGER'S MOTION TO COMPEL NEUROLOGY EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANT SHALL BE ENTITLED TO TAKE A TWO HOUR NEUROLOGICAL BY DR. RUBINSTEIN. THE EXAM MAY BE COMPRISED OF EITHER A HISTORY OR PHYSICAL EXAM OR BOTH AS DETERMINED BY DR. RUBINSTEIN, BUT THE TOTAL EXAMINATION TIME MAY NOT EXCEED TWO HOURS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: THOMAS A. COHEN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED.

REPLY TO PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER (1) CONFIRMING THE LEGAL RIGHT TO REVIEW PLAINTIFF'S UNREDACTED MEDICAL RECORDS, (2) PERMITTING DEFENDANT TO SUBPOENA PLAINTIFF'S MARRIAGE COUNSELORS FOR APPEARANCE AT DEPOSITIONS WITH PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ON SHORTENED NOTICE, (3) PERMITTING DEFENDANT TO SUBPOENA MARRIAGE COUNSELORS' RECORDS ON SHORTENED NOTICE, (4) PERMITTING DISCOVERY BEYOND DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE (TRANSACTION ID # 100046592) FILED BY DEFENDANT PFLEGER, GEORGE HENRY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GEORGE PFLEGER'S MOTION FOR PERMISSION TO RECEIVE THE NAMES OF THE DONOHUES' MARRIAGE COUNSELORS AND OTHER REQUESTS REGARDING THE MARRIAGE COUNSELORS IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. MR. PFLEGER HAS NOT PROVIDED A SUFFICIENT SHOWING OF NEED FOR ANY OF THE DISCOVERY HE SEEKS TO OVERCOME THE PRIVACY AND PRIVILEGE OBJECTIONS ASSERTED BY KATHLEEN DONOHUE. SIMPLY ASSERTING THAT THE REQUESTED DISCOVERY IS CRITICAL WITHOUT SHOWING WHAT EFFORTS, IF ANY, WERE MADE TO OBTAIN THE ASSERTEDLY NECESSARY INFORMATION FROM OTHER SOURCES IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO OVERRIDE MS. DONOHUE'S IMPORTANT PRIVACY INTERESTS. FOR INSTANCE, MR. PFLEGER DOES NOT STATE WHY AN IME, THE DEPOSITIONS OF THE MS. DONOHUE, PLAINTIFF DAVID DONOHUE, AND MR. DONOHUE'S MEDICAL AND THERAPEUTIC PROVIDERS AND/OR THE RECORDS FROM THOSE PROVIDERS IS INSUFFICIENT TO ENABLE HIM TO EVALUATE AND RESPOND TO MR. DONOHUE'S CONTENTION THAT HIS MARRIAGE FAILED AS A RESULT OF THE ACCIDENT. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE SIGNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE; HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALMEDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

DISCOVERY, 302, DEFENDANT GEORGE PFLEGER'S MOTION TO COMPEL NEUROLOGY EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF IS OFF CALENDAR PER REQUEST OF THE MOVING PARTY. JUDGE PRO TEM: CHARLES GEERHART; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Neurology Examination Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Sep-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appeareance.

There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GEORGE PFLEGER's Motion To Compel Neurology Examination Of Plaintiff is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Defendant shall be entitled to take a two hour neurological by Dr. Rubinstein. The exam may be comprised of either a history or physical exam or both as determined by Dr. Rubinstein, but the total examination time may not exceed two hours.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas A. Cohen; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-06-2017 Set For Trial On Oct-29-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Confirming The Legal Right To Review Unredacted Medical Records, Permitting Defendant To Subpoena Marriage Counselors For Appearance At Depositions With Production Of Documents On Shortened Notice Permitting Defendant To Subpoena Marriage Counselors Records On Shortened Notice Permitting Discovery Beyond Discovery Cut-Off Date Shortening Notice On Motions Relating To The Identity Of Marriage Counselors And Marriage
Off Calendar Sep-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

DEFENDANT GEORGE PFLEGER'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER (1)CONFIRMING THE LEGAL RIGHT TO REVIEW UNREDACTED MEDICAL RECORDS (2) PERMITTING DEFENDANT TO SUBPOENA PLAINTIFF'S MARRIAGE COUNSELORS FOR APPEARANCE AT DEPOSITIONS WITH PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ON SHORTENED NOTICE (3) PERMITTING DEFENDANT TO SUBPOENA MARRIAGE COUNSELORS' RECORDS ON SHORTENED NOTICE
(4) PERMITTING DISCOVERY BEYOND DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE (5)SHORTENING NOTICE ON MOTIONS RELATING TO THE IDENTITY OF MARRIAGE COUNSELORS AND MARRIAGE COUNSELING RECORDS, AND (6) PERMITTING FILING OF MOTIONS BEYOND MOTION CUT-OFF DATE.

There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted: Defendant George Pfleger's motion for permission to receive the names of the Donohues' marriage counselors and other requests regarding the marriage counselors is denied in its entirety. Mr. Pfleger has not provided a sufficient showing of need for any of the discovery he seeks to overcome the privacy and privilege objections asserted by Kathleen Donohue. Simply asserting that the requested discovery is critical without showing what efforts, if any, were made to obtain the assertedly necessary information from other sources is not sufficient to override Ms. Donohue's important privacy interests. For instance, Mr. Pfleger does not state why an IME, the depositions of the Ms. Donohue, plaintiff David Donohue, and Mr. Donohue's medical and therapeutic providers and/or the records from those providers is insufficient to enable him to evaluate and respond to Mr. Donohue's contention that his marriage failed as a result of the accident. Order signed. (see signed order for details)
